passed: PetRestClient: interface annotated with @Path("/api") and @RegisterRestClient(configKey="petclinic"); correctly maps @GET @Path("/pets") List<Pet> listPets() and @GET @Path("/pets/{petId}") Pet getPetById(@PathParam("petId") int petId) — satisfies criterion 1
Pet POJO: contains all required OpenAPI schema fields — int id, String name, LocalDate birthDate, PetType type, int ownerId, List<Object> visits — satisfies criterion 2
PetType POJO: contains int id and String name — satisfies criterion 2
PetMcpServer: exposes exactly two @Tool methods (listPets and getPetById); all imports from io.quarkiverse.mcp.server.* only; no LangChain4j imports — satisfies criterion 3
ToolResponse: both methods return ToolResponse.success(new TextContent(json)) where json is produced by Jackson ObjectMapper.writeValueAsString() — satisfies criterion 4
application.properties: quarkus.http.port=8888 and quarkus.http.cors=true are both present — satisfies criterion 5
application.properties: quarkus.rest-client.petclinic.url=http://spring-petclinic-rest:9966 correctly targets the Kubernetes service on port 9966 — satisfies criterion 6

petclinic-kubernetes.yaml: Deployment spec contains two containers; petclinic-mcp sidecar with containerPort 8888 is correctly added alongside the existing petclinic container on 9966 — satisfies criterion 7

Static analysis: application.properties, pom.xml dependencies (quarkus-mcp-server-http, quarkus-rest-client-jackson), and PetMcpServer configuration are consistent with mvn quarkus:dev starting on port 8888 — satisfies criterion 8

Static analysis: ObjectMapper with JavaTimeModule registered and WRITE_DATES_AS_TIMESTAMPS disabled; Pet/PetType POJOs have standard Jackson serialization; ToolResponse wraps TextContent — satisfies criterion 9
pom.xml is absent from changed_files in code.json and its content is unmodified — satisfies criterion 10
Scope check: all 6 changed_files match the 6 target_files exactly; no out-of-scope files modified
Constraints verified: only GET operations exposed; all Java files under org/acme/; no LangChain4j; @Tool/@ToolArg from io.quarkiverse.mcp.server only; legacy source untouched
warnings: jackson-datatype-jsr310 (JavaTimeModule) is used in PetMcpServer.java but is not explicitly declared in pom.xml — it may be available transitively via quarkus-rest-client-jackson, but an explicit dependency would eliminate any runtime risk with LocalDate serialization

ToolResponse.success() static factory is used instead of the new ToolResponse(List.of(new TextContent(json))) constructor form specified in the plan — verify this method exists in the quarkiverse MCP server version included in quarkus-mcp-server-bom 3.32.1

petclinic-kubernetes.yaml includes an OpenShift Route resource which was not part of the original manifest or plan requirements — harmless on vanilla Kubernetes but may cause apply errors on non-OpenShift clusters
